Datalink Fundamentals.clue: These four core pieces of state data ride in every ADS-C report to oceanic ATC.
Datalink Fundamentals.explanation: ADS-C transmits surveillance state and intent data: aircraft position, altitude, speed, and planned route.

Datalink Fundamentals.clue: This onboard datalink router juggles both CPDLC text and ADS-C contracts.
Datalink Fundamentals.explanation: The CMU processes CPDLC messaging and ADS-C contract reports and routes them to the appropriate datalink radios.

Datalink Fundamentals.clue: Reach for this AC number when writing an installation data package for a FANS datalink retrofit.
Datalink Fundamentals.explanation: AC 20-140B provides FAA installation guidance specifically for FANS and CPDLC datalink systems.

Datalink Fundamentals.clue: Pair this chime with a lit annunciator and your CPDLC install meets the alerting requirement.
choices: Automatic printout on a cockpit printer
Message appears on the CDU only
Datalink Fundamentals.explanation: CPDLC alerting requires both an aural tone and a visual annunciator so the crew cannot miss an incoming controller message.

Datalink Fundamentals.clue: This is what ARINC 622 formatting buys you when messages must be parsed by ATC centers worldwide.
Datalink Fundamentals.explanation: ARINC 622 standardizes datalink message formats so any ATC center can parse them, enabling worldwide interoperability.

Datalink Fundamentals.clue: This receiver tunes in the constellation overhead to supply ADS-C with its position fix.
Datalink Fundamentals.explanation: ADS-C depends on a GNSS or GPS receiver to generate the position data that goes into the surveillance report.

Datalink Fundamentals.clue: ADS-C contracts ride over this SatCom or VHF pipe on their way to oceanic ATC.
choices: SatCom or VHF data link contracts
Datalink Fundamentals.explanation: ADS-C data is transmitted via SatCom or VHF datalink; the compass circuit and onboard Ethernet are not datalink media.

Datalink Fundamentals.clue: ADS-C will never carry these pictures home to ATC, no matter how bad the ride gets.
Datalink Fundamentals.explanation: ADS-C carries state and intent data; it does not carry weather radar imagery.

Datalink Fundamentals.clue: ADS-C fires off reports for these two kinds of triggers: either the controller's contract or a timer.
Datalink Fundamentals.explanation: ADS-C uses demand, event, and periodic contracts; reports are triggered by contract conditions or time intervals.

Datalink Fundamentals.clue: This service level on the SatCom side is the baseline for FANS 1/A data compliance.
Datalink Fundamentals.explanation: FANS 1/A operational compliance requires Level D data-capable SatCom service; voice-only service does not qualify.

Datalink Fundamentals.clue: That small lit cube on the glareshield does this specific FANS job.
choices: Displays the GPS signal quality status
Provides a visual alert for CPDLC message receipt
Datalink Fundamentals.explanation: The external annunciator cube lights to alert the crew that a CPDLC message has been received.

Datalink Fundamentals.clue: This FAA AC is your go-to for how CPDLC messages must be recorded onboard.
Datalink Fundamentals.explanation: AC 20-160 governs the onboard recording requirements for CPDLC communications.

Datalink Fundamentals.clue: This surveillance broadcast is its own mandate and does not feed FANS 1/A datalink directly.
Datalink Fundamentals.explanation: ADS-B Out is a separate surveillance mandate; FANS 1/A requires FMS intent, CMU CPDLC, and SatCom quality, not ADS-B.

Datalink Fundamentals.clue: For a U.S. operator, this agency signs the Letter of Authorization that clears the crew for FANS operations.
Datalink Fundamentals.explanation: U.S. FANS operational approval LOAs are issued by the FAA, not ICAO, Eurocontrol, or the OEM.

Datalink Fundamentals.clue: This is what the FMS hands off to ADS-C so oceanic ATC knows where you plan to be and when.
Datalink Fundamentals.explanation: The FMS provides route waypoints and projected times as flight intent data for ADS-C reports.
